target.title: fix(feishu): degrade gracefully when card table count exceeds platform limit (230099/11310)
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Feishu outbound card delivery when Interactive Card rendering hits the CardKit table-count limit (`230099` / `11310`, `table number over limit`).

Instead of failing the outbound send, OpenClaw now degrades the affected card send to the existing plain-text Feishu message path.

## What changed

- Added `card-error.ts` to parse and classify CardKit table-limit errors.
- Wrapped two outbound card paths with table-limit-specific fallback:
  - `sendOutboundText()` → `sendMarkdownCardFeishu()` → fallback to `sendMessageFeishu()`.
  - `feishuOutbound.sendText()` → `sendStructuredCardFeishu()` → fallback to `sendOutboundText(..., forcePlainText: true)`.
- Non-table-limit errors are re-thrown unchanged.
- Thread routing is preserved on fallback paths.
- Added focused tests for parser behavior and fallback behavior.

## Scope

This PR intentionally covers outbound sends only.

Related Feishu surfaces such as reply-dispatcher / streaming card fallback are left as follow-up work and should be coordinated with #43690 / #50429 if maintainers want a broader consolidated fix.

## Review feedback addressed

- Removed misleading `errCode` fallback to outer `code`.
- Removed unrelated duplicated native-card test block.
- Kept the PR as a focused single-commit diff.

## Validation

- `oxlint` passed for all changed Feishu files.
- CI is running on the PR.
